description: Capcom's action-RPG franchise built around hunting massive creatures, crafting gear from their parts, and hunting bigger creatures. Started in 2004 on PS2, found its mainstream audience with Monster Hunter: World (2018), and became one of Capcom's best-selling series with over 100 million units sold.

media.title: Monster Hunter
media.description: The PS2 original that started it all. Hunt monsters, harvest parts, craft gear. Online multiplayer via PS2 Network Adaptor (now defunct).
media.notes: PCSX2 required. Online dead. The foundation — playable but very rough by modern standards.

media.title: Monster Hunter Freedom
media.description: PSP port of the original with added content and offline-friendly design. The game that defined the franchise in Japan.
media.notes: Core: ppsspp. Starting point for many Japanese fans.

media.title: Monster Hunter 2 (Dos)
media.description: PS2 sequel. Japan-only release. Introduced day/night cycles, seasons, and many monsters that became series staples. Never officially localized.
media.notes: PCSX2 + fan translation patch required. Japan-only.

media.title: Monster Hunter Freedom 2
media.description: PSP port of MH2 Dos with Western localization. Added the Felyne kitchen and expanded quest roster.
media.notes: Core: ppsspp.

media.title: Monster Hunter Freedom Unite
media.description: Expanded edition of Freedom 2 with hundreds of hours of content. The definitive PSP Monster Hunter. Available on iOS/Android.
media.notes: Core: ppsspp. Also on iOS/Android (paid, offline). The PSP pinnacle.

media.title: Monster Hunter Tri
media.description: Wii-exclusive reboot introducing underwater combat, Lagiacrus, and the Switch Axe weapon. Online servers shut down in 2013.
media.notes: Core: dolphin. Online defunct. Best entry point for Wii — clean, stylish reboot.

media.title: Monster Hunter 3 Ultimate
media.description: Expanded Tri with a massive new content set, dual-platform play, and the addition of Brachydios. WiiU online servers shut down in 2013. The definitive Tri experience.
media.notes: Core: dolphin (Wii/WiiU), Citra (3DS). Cross-platform local co-op supported.

media.title: Monster Hunter 3 Ultimate HD
media.description: WiiU retail release with HD visuals. Same content as 3 Ultimate. Online was available via Nintendo Network until 2013.
media.notes: Core: dolphin. Covered by the 3U entry above — same game.

media.title: Monster Hunter 4
media.description: 3DS-exclusive introducing mounting mechanics, vertical terrain, and online multiplayer via Nintendo Wi-Fi. Japan-only; preceded 4 Ultimate in the West.
media.notes: Citra. Japan-only — 4 Ultimate is the Western version.

media.title: Monster Hunter 4 Ultimate
media.description: Expanded 4 with Charge Blade and Insect Glaive weapons, Apex monsters, and a full story campaign. The fan-favorite portable entry.
media.notes: Core: Citra. Nintendo online servers for 3DS shut down May 2024 — fan servers (Pretendo) active.

media.title: Monster Hunter Generations
media.description: Celebration game for the 10th anniversary. Introduced Hunting Styles, Hunting Arts, and the Fated Four elder dragons. Japan title: Monster Hunter X (Cross).
media.notes: Core: Citra. Nintendo 3DS online dead — Pretendo fan servers active.

media.title: Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate
media.description: Expanded Generations with G-Rank, Bloodbath Diablos, and two additional Deviants per returning monster. Japan title: MH XX. Switch version available in the West.
media.notes: Core: Citra (3DS), Switch emulator or native. Massive endgame, overwhelming for newcomers.

media.title: Monster Hunter: World
media.description: The series reboot that went fully open-world. Seamless zones, ecological ecosystems, and co-op drop-in. Became the best-selling game in Capcom's history. Best entry point for newcomers.
media.notes: PC via Steam. The definitive modern entry point. ⭐ Start here.

media.title: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne
media.description: Massive expansion to World adding the Hoarfrost Reach, Master Rank difficulty, Clutch Claw mechanic, and Velkhana elder dragon.
media.notes: Requires MH: World. Play immediately after completing World's main story.

media.title: Monster Hunter Rise
media.description: Switch-first entry introducing the Wirebug traversal system, Rampage quests, and Palamute dog companions. Faster and more arcade-y than World.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Switch native. Great on both platforms.

media.title: Monster Hunter Rise: Sunbreak
media.description: Paid expansion to Rise with Master Rank, the Citadel locale, and the Three Lords elder dragons (Malzeno, Lunagaron, Garangolm).
media.notes: Requires Rise. PC via Steam. Significantly harder than base Rise.

media.title: Monster Hunter Wilds
media.description: The next mainline entry. Dynamic weather ecosystems, new weapon mechanics, and the Forbidden Lands setting. Highest pre-order count in Capcom history.
media.notes: PC via Steam / PS5 / Xbox Series X. The current-gen flagship.

media.title: Monster Hunter Diary: Poka Poka Airou Village
media.description: Felyne-centric PSP spinoff. Play as an Airou (Palico cat) in a chibi village. Japan-only, no official localization.
media.notes: Core: ppsspp + fan translation. Cute, low-stakes spinoff.

media.title: Monster Hunter Diary: Poka Poka Airou Village G
media.description: Expanded edition of the Airou Village diary game. Japan-only.
media.notes: Core: ppsspp + fan translation.

media.title: Monster Hunter Diary: Poka Poka Airou Village DX
media.description: 3DS version of the Airou village diary spinoff. Japan-only.
media.notes: Citra + fan translation.

media.title: Monster Hunter Dynamic Hunting
media.description: iOS-only side-scrolling brawler. Hunt monsters in simplified 2D combat. Delisted from the App Store.
media.notes: Defunct — delisted. iOS-only. Never on Android.

media.title: Monster Hunter Explore
media.description: Mobile free-to-play spinoff with gacha elements. Featured crossover events and a large roster. Japan-only. Service terminated 2019.
media.notes: Defunct — servers shut down September 2019. Japan-only.

media.title: Monster Hunter Freedom Unite (iOS/Android)
media.description: Port of Monster Hunter Freedom Unite for mobile. Paid game, playable offline. Full PSP content on phone.
media.notes: 📱 Fully mobile. iOS and Android. Still available as of 2024.

media.title: Monster Hunter Now
media.description: Niantic's location-based Monster Hunter mobile game. Real-world AR hunting, Pokémon GO-style. Hunt monsters tied to real-world locations.
media.notes: 📱 Mobile AR. iOS and Android. Free-to-play. Active service as of 2025.

media.title: Monster Hunter Stories
media.description: JRPG spinoff where you ride monsters instead of hunt them. Rider protagonist hatches monster eggs. Bright, accessible, story-driven.
media.notes: Core: Citra (3DS). Also on iOS/Android (paid). Also on PC via Steam (2021 remaster).

media.title: Monster Hunter Stories 2: Wings of Ruin
media.description: JRPG sequel following the grandson of the original Stories protagonist. Rated highly — the best entry point into the spinoff line.
media.notes: PC via Steam / Switch native. ⭐ Best Stories entry.

media.title: Monster Hunter Online
media.description: PC MMO developed with Tencent for the Chinese market. Featured unique monsters (Zinogre variants, Espinas) that later appeared in mainline games. Service terminated 2019.
media.notes: Defunct — servers shut down August 2019. China-only. Private server efforts exist.

media.title: Monster Hunter Riders
media.description: Mobile RPG spinoff in the Stories universe with gacha mechanics. Japan-only. Service terminated 2022.
media.notes: Defunct — servers shut down March 2022. Japan-only.

media.title: Monster Hunter: Battle Quest
media.description: Western-market mobile title. Card-based combat with Monster Hunter monsters. Delisted.
media.notes: Defunct — delisted. Western mobile only.

media.title: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne Mobile (beta)
media.description: Canceled mobile port of Iceborne that entered limited beta testing in Japan before being terminated. Never fully released.
media.notes: Canceled — beta only, never shipped.

media.title: Monster Hunter (2004) — Nintendo DS tech demo
media.description: Unreleased Nintendo DS tech demo / prototype. Capcom demonstrated Monster Hunter running on DS hardware but canceled it in favor of PSP.
media.notes: Canceled — never released. Prototype only.

media.title: Monster Hunter Stories (anime)
media.description: Anime adaptation of the Stories JRPG. Follows Lute, a Rider who partners with a Rathalos named Rathalos. Bright, adventure-focused, good for younger viewers.
media.notes: Streamed on Crunchyroll. Watch after or alongside Stories the game.

media.title: Monster Hunter (film)
media.description: Paul W.S. Anderson live-action film. U.S. Army soldiers are transported to the Monster Hunter world and must fight to survive. Stars Milla Jovovich and Tony Jaa.
media.notes: Available on digital/physical. Not required watching — purely optional.

media.title: Monster Hunter: Legends of the Guild
media.description: Netflix CGI animated film. Prequel story to Monster Hunter: World featuring Aiden, a young hunter, protecting his village from an Elder Dragon invasion.
media.notes: Netflix. Watch before or after World — set in a loose prequel timeline.
